question: What is the full list of hook events a charm can observe? List them all.

claude_output.result:
Here is the full list of hook events a charm can observe, organized by category:

---

## Core Lifecycle Hooks

| Hook | When it fires |
|---|---|
| `install` | Once, before any other hook, when the charm is first installed |
| `start` | After initial configuration is complete and the unit is ready |
| `config-changed` | After `install`, after `upgrade-charm`, and whenever the application config changes |
| `update-status` | Periodically at a regular interval (default: every 5 minutes) |
| `upgrade-charm` | After new charm code is deployed to the unit |
| `stop` | When a unit begins being removed |
| `remove` | The very last hook a unit sees before it is torn down |

---

## Leadership Hooks

| Hook | When it fires |
|---|---|
| `leader-elected` | When this unit is elected the leader of the application |
| `leader-settings-changed` | When the leader changes or when the leader writes new settings (fires on non-leader units) |

---

## Relation Hooks
*(where `<endpoint>` is the name of the relation endpoint in `metadata.yaml`)*

| Hook | When it fires |
|---|---|
| `<endpoint>-relation-created` | When two applications are related |
| `<endpoint>-relation-joined` | When a remote unit joins the relation |
| `<endpoint>-relation-changed` | When a remote unit's relation data changes |
| `<endpoint>-relation-departed` | When a remote unit leaves the relation |
| `<endpoint>-relation-broken` | When the relation itself is removed |

---

## Storage Hooks
*(where `<storage>` is the name of the storage in `metadata.yaml`)*

| Hook | When it fires |
|---|---|
| `<storage>-storage-attached` | When a storage instance becomes available to the unit |
| `<storage>-storage-detaching` | Just before a storage instance is removed |

---

## Secret Hooks *(Juju secrets)*

| Hook | When it fires |
|---|---|
| `secret-changed` | When the secret owner publishes a new revision (fires on observer units) |
| `secret-rotate` | When a secret's rotation policy triggers (fires on the owning unit) |
| `secret-expired` | When a secret revision reaches its expiry time (fires on the owning unit) |
| `secret-remove` | When an old secret revision can be safely deleted (fires on the owning unit) |

---

## Workload / Pebble Hooks *(Kubernetes / sidecar charms only)*
*(where `<container>` is the name of the container in `metadata.yaml`)*

| Hook | When it fires |
|---|---|
| `<container>-pebble-ready` | When the Pebble API in the container becomes available |
| `<container>-pebble-custom-notice` | When a custom Pebble notice is received from the container |
| `<container>-pebble-check-failed` | When a Pebble health check transitions to failed |
| `<container>-pebble-check-recovered` | When a previously failed Pebble health check passes again |

---

## Upgrade Series Hooks *(machine charms only)*

| Hook | When it fires |
|---|---|
| `pre-series-upgrade` | Before the host machine's OS series is upgraded |
| `post-series-upgrade` | After the host machine's OS series upgrade completes |

---

That's the complete set of observable hook events. The parameterized hooks (`<endpoint>-*`, `<storage>-*`, `<container>-*`) are instantiated at runtime based on the names you declare in your charm's `metadata.yaml`.
